maize_diseases_Abiotic_disease-D.suggestion: Check soil moisture, nutrient levels, and environmental conditions. Avoid excessive fertilization or chemical use. Ensure proper irrigation.

maize_diseases_Curvularia-D.suggestion: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Apply appropriate fungicides. Ensure proper spacing and avoid waterlogging.

maize_diseases_Healthy_leaf.suggestion: Plant is healthy. Continue regular monitoring, proper watering, fertilization, and pest management.

maize_diseases_Helminthosporiosis-D.suggestion: Apply fungicides during early infection. Remove infected debris. Use resistant maize varieties.

maize_diseases_Rust-D.suggestion: Spray recommended fungicides. Ensure good air circulation and remove infected leaves. Rotate crops.

maize_diseases_Stripe-D.suggestion: Use certified disease-free seeds. Apply fungicides and maintain crop rotation. Monitor regularly for early signs.

maize_diseases_Virosis-D.suggestion: Remove infected plants immediately. Control insect vectors. Avoid planting in infected areas for a season.

maize_pests_Chenille l├⌐gionnaire-P.suggestion: Handpick larvae if possible. Use Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) sprays or recommended insecticides. Encourage natural predators.

maize_pests_Pucerons-P.suggestion: Use neem oil or insecticidal soaps. Encourage ladybugs and lacewings as natural predators.

maize_pests_activities_Chenille l├⌐gionn.suggestion: Monitor larval activity. Apply Bt-based biopesticides or approved chemical insecticides. Remove heavily infested plants.

onion_diseases_Alternaria_D.suggestion: Remove infected leaves and bulbs. Apply appropriate fungicides. Ensure crop rotation and avoid overhead irrigation.

onion_diseases_Bulb_blight-D.suggestion: Use disease-free sets. Apply fungicides. Avoid planting in wet, compacted soils and remove infected bulbs.

onion_diseases_Fusarium-D.suggestion: Use resistant varieties. Avoid waterlogging and practice crop rotation. Remove infected plants.

onion_diseases_Healthy_leaf.suggestion: Maintain regular care, proper irrigation, and pest monitoring to keep onions healthy.

onion_diseases_Virosis-D.suggestion: Remove infected plants. Control aphid populations which spread the virus. Avoid replanting in the same area immediately.

onion_pests_Chenilles-P.suggestion: Apply recommended insecticides or biopesticides. Handpick caterpillars if feasible. Encourage natural predators.

tomato_diseases_Bacterial_floundering_d.suggestion: Remove and destroy infected plants. Avoid overhead watering. Apply copper-based bactericides and practice crop rotation.

tomato_diseases_Blossom_end_rot_d.suggestion: Maintain consistent soil moisture. Apply calcium-rich fertilizers. Avoid sudden changes in watering.

tomato_diseases_Mite_d.suggestion: Spray miticides or neem oil. Maintain proper plant spacing and remove heavily infested leaves.

tomato_diseases_alternaria_d.suggestion: Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicides. Ensure proper ventilation and avoid overhead irrigation.

tomato_diseases_alternaria_mite_d.suggestion: Combine mite control with fungicide application. Keep plants well-pruned and spaced. Monitor regularly.

tomato_diseases_exces_nitrogen_d.suggestion: Avoid over-fertilization. Maintain balanced nutrient supply. Monitor growth and adjust fertilizers accordingly.

tomato_diseases_fusarium_d.suggestion: Use resistant varieties. Remove infected plants. Practice crop rotation and solarize soil if possible.

tomato_diseases_healthy_fruit.suggestion: Fruits are healthy. Maintain proper watering, nutrient management, and pest monitoring.

tomato_diseases_healthy_leaf.suggestion: Leaves are healthy. Continue good cultural practices and regular inspection for pests and diseases.

tomato_diseases_sunburn_d.suggestion: Provide shade during peak sunlight. Mulch around plants to retain moisture. Avoid water stress.

tomato_diseases_tomato_late_blight_d.suggestion: Remove and destroy infected leaves and plants. Apply fungicides and practice crop rotation. Avoid overhead watering.

tomato_diseases_virosis_d.suggestion: Remove infected plants. Control insect vectors (whiteflies, aphids). Avoid planting susceptible varieties in the same field.

tomato_pests_helicoverpa_armigera_p.suggestion: Use pheromone traps or biopesticides. Apply recommended insecticides if infestation is high.

tomato_pests_tuta_absoluta_p.suggestion: Install pheromone traps. Use biological control agents. Apply insecticides carefully and monitor regularly.
